displayName: General Ledger — Scrollable Table with Column Filtering

decisions.rationale: Purpose-built for virtual scrolling, column filtering, sorting, and server-side pagination. Battle-tested at 100k+ rows. Required for millions of journal entries.

decisions.decision: Add full server-side filtering to GET /api/v1/ledger/journal-entries
decisions.rationale: Current endpoint only supports page/size/sort. Client-side filtering cannot handle millions of rows. Need account, date range, description LIKE, entryNumber, transactionType filters.

decisions.decision: Expandable rows — one summary row per journal entry, click to expand debit/credit lines
decisions.rationale: Matches Xero/QuickBooks UX pattern. Cleaner at scale — reduces visual noise. A 4-line journal entry shows as 1 row until expanded.

decisions.decision: Column header row inputs — small text inputs / dropdowns embedded directly in each column header cell
decisions.rationale: Compact, Excel-like pattern. Standard for accounting/ledger tables. Allows column-specific search without a separate filter bar.
Render only visible rows for performance. Sticky header keeps column labels and filter inputs visible while scrolling.

decisions.decision: Server-side pagination with infinite scroll (Load More / auto-fetch on scroll)
decisions.rationale: Cannot load millions of rows at once. TanStack Virtual + server-side pagination fetches pages as user scrolls.

constraints: No new Java modules — backend changes go in sacco-ledger
TanStack Table dependency scoped to Ledger page initially; other pages keep plain HTML tables
All monetary columns use BigDecimal on backend, formatted client-side
Backend filtering must use indexed columns for query performance
Journal entries with journalLines are fetched as nested objects (existing shape), expanded client-side
Must preserve existing API response shape (PageResponse wrapper)
Column filter inputs must debounce server requests (300-500ms)
Sticky header must work within the app shell layout (sidebar + main content scroll area)
openQuestions: Should we add database indexes on journal_entry.description (for LIKE search) or rely on ILIKE with existing indexes?
Should expanded row detail show the line-level description field or just account/debit/credit?
Should date range filter use the existing MonthPicker or a from/to date pair?
Should we add CSV/PDF export to the ledger page (like Audit has)?
Virtual scroll container height — fixed pixel height or calc(100vh - header) fluid?

research.findings: Sticky headers, column-level filters, virtual scrolling, zebra striping, and customizable columns are standard for enterprise data tables. Server-side filtering required for datasets over 1,000 rows.

TanStack Table provides headless UI for column filtering, sorting, pagination. TanStack Virtual handles row virtualization. Supports server-side filtering with manual pagination control. Tested to 100k+ rows.

Accounting software uses expandable journal entries, date range filters, account dropdowns, and search-by-description. Xero emphasizes clean interfaces with searchable menus. QuickBooks uses more granular control but with cluttered UI.
